methodsAttempted.query: site:pitt.edu University of Pittsburgh generative AI academic integrity assessment students policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced official University of Pittsburgh Teaching Center guidance on teaching with generative AI, encouraging academic integrity, a Pitt Research PDF, and an Office of Medical Education academic-integrity PDF.
Firecrawl search again surfaced Teaching Center guidance pages as the strongest direct official sources for AI syllabus language and academic-integrity handling.

The teaching-with-generative-AI page linked to AI/data-privacy guidance, academic integrity guidance, the University Academic Integrity Guidelines PDF, and a Canvas sample-assignment repository requiring sign-in.

artifacts.summary: The crawl found official University of Pittsburgh Teaching Center guidance covering generative-AI syllabus permission levels, AI output attribution, instructor communication of course AI expectations, AI detector non-support, and data privacy/security cautions for free GenAI tools. A Canvas sample-assignment repository was login-gated and the general Academic Integrity Guidelines PDF was not used for AI-specific claims.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:pitt.edu University of Pittsburgh generative AI academic integrity assessment students policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official University Center for Teaching and Learning page with suggested syllabus statements by generative-AI permission level, attribution language, teaching uses, and ethical-use guidance.

Official Teaching Center academic-integrity guidance with direct statements about AI detectors, Turnitin AI detection, and following standard University Academic Integrity Guidelines for suspected AI misuse.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI and Data Privacy and Security
artifacts.queryUsed: Teaching with Generative AI page data privacy implications link
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Teaching Center guidance on GenAI data privacy/security risks for research, publishing, instruction, and administrative/service activities.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University of Pittsburgh Guidelines on Academic Integrity
artifacts.queryUsed: Teaching with Generative AI syllabus statement link to Guidelines on Academic Integrity PDF
artifacts.verificationNotes: The PDF is an official general academic-integrity policy and was reachable, but the staged claims use the Teaching Center pages for direct AI-specific language.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Sample Assignments That Integrate Generative AI, Created by Pitt Faculty
artifacts.queryUsed: Teaching with Generative AI sample assignment repository link
artifacts.verificationNotes: The sample-assignment repository is a Pitt Canvas course that requires institutional login, so it was not accessed or used as evidence.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The PDF is the University Guidelines on Academic Integrity linked by the AI syllabus examples, but the current staged claims require direct AI-specific Teaching Center wording.
artifacts.notes: Documented as official background; not used for standalone AI claims.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The Teaching Center describes the linked repository as Sample Assignments That Integrate Generative AI, Created by Pitt Faculty, but the Canvas course requires login.
artifacts.notes: No Canvas login bypass was attempted.

targets.sourceTitle: AI and Data Privacy and Security

stopConditions: Stop after direct official Teaching Center guidance pages are captured.
Do not use search snippets, external syllabus examples, or third-party AI detector articles as evidence.
Do not access Canvas or other login-gated content.
Do not promote staged claims as canonical facts.

artifacts.errorReason: Canvas course content requires institutional sign-in; no login bypass attempted.

artifacts.claimText: University of Pittsburgh Teaching Center guidance strongly recommends that instructors include an AI syllabus statement that clearly communicates expectations to students in all courses.

citation.publisher: University of Pittsburgh University Center for Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: We also strongly recommend including an AI syllabus statement that will clearly communicate your expectations to your students in all your courses.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: We also strongly recommend including an AI syllabus statement that will clearly communicate your expectations to your students in all your courses.

artifacts.claimText: University of Pittsburgh Teaching Center suggested syllabus language includes a no-use option that prohibits unauthorized collaboration or use of ChatGPT or other generative AI applications.

citation.publisher: University of Pittsburgh University Center for Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All work completed and/or submitted in this course must be your own, completed in accordance with the University's Guidelines on Academic Integrity. You may not engage in unauthorized collaboration or make use of ChatGPT or any other generative AI applications at any time.

artifacts.claimText: University of Pittsburgh Teaching Center suggested syllabus language says broader generative-AI use may be permitted or encouraged within specified guidelines, but AI-generated material that informed student work should be cited and unattributed AI-generated content qualifies as academic dishonesty.

citation.publisher: University of Pittsburgh University Center for Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of Generative AI tools, including ChatGPT, is encouraged/permitted in this course for students who wish to use them. You may choose to use AI tools to help brainstorm assignments or projects or to revise existing work you have written. However, to adhere to scholarly values, students must cite any AI-generated material that informed their work.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The use of Generative AI tools, including ChatGPT, is encouraged/permitted in this course for students who wish to use them. You may choose to use AI tools to help brainstorm assignments or projects or to revise existing work you have written. However, to adhere to scholarly values, students must cite any AI-generated material that informed their work.

artifacts.notes: Claim is limited to the Teaching Center's recommendation about AI syllabus statements and does not assert a binding university-wide student rule.
Claim describes a suggested no-use syllabus statement, not a universal University prohibition.
Claim is framed as suggested syllabus language for broader permitted use and preserves the attribution condition.
Claim directly tracks the Teaching Center sentence on not using GenAI to grade student work and the linked data-privacy caution.
Claim is limited to the Teaching Center's support/endorsement position on AI-detection tools.
Claim directly reflects the privacy/security guidance about free GenAI platforms and non-public information.

artifacts.title: University of Pittsburgh generative AI staged source review
artifacts.summary: Staged review of official University of Pittsburgh sources found public Teaching Center guidance on generative-AI syllabus statements, course-level permission examples, attribution for AI-generated material, instructor use of AI in teaching, AI detector limitations, and privacy/security cautions for free GenAI tools. Claims remain unpromoted agent-reviewed candidates.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Teaching Center pages are guidance resources; claims are not presented as binding university-wide policy beyond the exact evidence wording.
A Canvas sample-assignment repository required login and was not accessed or used as evidence.
The general University Academic Integrity Guidelines PDF was documented as official background but not used for AI-specific claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
